steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Each term in $42x^2+7x+7$ has a greatest common factor of $7$. Factoring $7$ out of every term keeps the expression equivalent.
$$42x^2+7x+7=7(6x^2+x+1)$$

steps.title: Match the factored form
steps.content: Choice A has an extra factor of $x$, choice B loses the constant term, and choice D uses the wrong common factor. The equivalent expression is $7(6x^2+x+1)$. The answer is C.
